input.background: Suppose that a system is restored to "as good as new" periodically at intervals of time $T$. So the system renews itself at time $T, 2 T, \ldots$. Define system reliability under preventive maintenance (PM) as $R_M(t)=\operatorname{Pr}\{\text { failure has not occurred by time } \mathrm{t}\}$. In other words, the system survives to time $t$ if and only if it survives every PM cycle $\{1,2, \ldots, k\}$ and a further time $(t-k T)$.
input.question: For a system with a failure time probability density function $f(t)=\lambda^2 t e^{-\lambda t} \text { for } t>0, \lambda>0$, obtain the system reliability under PM and system mean time to first failure.

output.answer: The system reliability under PM is $R_M(t)=[R(T)]^k R(t-k T) \text { for } k T<t<(k+1) T \text { and } k=0,1,2, \ldots$. The system mean time to first failure (MTTFF) is $M T T F F=\frac{\int_0^T R(u) d u}{1-R(T)}$, where $\int_0^T R(u) d u = \frac{2}{\lambda}\left(1-e^{-\lambda T}\right)-T e^{-\lambda T}$.
